faqs.question: What pantry pests are most common in Stapleton homes?
faqs.answer: Stapleton residents most frequently encounter Indian meal moths, flour beetles, grain weevils, and cigarette beetles. These pests infest dry goods like cereals, flour, rice, pasta, nuts, dried fruits, and pet food. Our comprehensive inspection identifies the exact pest species and all infested products to ensure complete elimination.

faqs.question: How quickly can you provide pantry pest control service in Stapleton?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service throughout Stapleton and Staten Island. Most pantry pest treatments take 1-2 hours depending on the size of your kitchen and extent of infestation. Our technicians serve all Stapleton neighborhoods, from the waterfront to areas near Silver Lake Park.

faqs.question: What does your pantry pest control service include?
faqs.answer: Our $350-$550 service includes a thorough inspection of all food storage areas, identification of infested products, targeted treatment using food-safe methods, crack and crevice application, pheromone trap placement, and detailed prevention recommendations. We also provide guidance on proper food storage and sanitation practices to prevent future infestations.

faqs.question: How can I prevent pantry pests from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Store all dry goods in airtight glass or hard plastic containers, regularly clean pantry shelves and corners, check expiration dates, inspect grocery items before storing them, and maintain proper ventilation in storage areas. We provide customized prevention tips based on your specific Stapleton home's layout and storage conditions during our service visit.
